name: Algorithms
description: Foundational ideas for analyzing and designing efficient algorithms.

group.definition: A notation describing the upper bound of an algorithm's running time or space as input size grows; the most common way to express **Time Complexity**.
A measure of how the number of operations an algorithm performs grows with input size, usually expressed with **Big O Notation**.
A strategy that breaks a problem into subproblems, solves them independently, and combines the results, often implemented with **Recursion**.
An approach that makes the locally optimal choice at each step; unlike **Dynamic Programming**, it never reconsiders earlier choices.
Solving problems by storing the results of overlapping subproblems to avoid the repeated work of plain **Recursion**.
A technique where a function solves a problem by calling itself on smaller subproblems, central to **Divide and Conquer**.
An efficient O(log n) **Divide and Conquer** search that repeatedly halves a sorted range to locate a target value.
Systematically visiting the nodes of a graph, commonly via breadth-first or depth-first search.
